roles.title: Software Engineer (SDE)
roles.description: You will solve coding problems and demonstrate strong logical thinking. This section measures your problem solving and coding efficiency.

questions.question: Can you explain the difference between an array and a linked list? When would you use one over the other?
What is the time complexity of binary search and why is it more efficient than linear search?
How would you implement a LRU cache? Describe the data structures you would use and the time complexity of operations.
Explain the concept of recursion and provide an example where it's more suitable than iteration.
Design a system for a URL shortener service like bit.ly. Discuss the architecture, database schema, and how you would handle scalability.
How would you optimize a slow database query? Walk through your troubleshooting process and various optimization techniques.
What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous programming? When would you use each?
Explain the concept of Big O notation. Why is it important for algorithm analysis?
What is a stack and how does it differ from a queue? Provide real-world examples.
How would you detect a cycle in a linked list? Describe multiple approaches.
Explain the difference between depth-first search (DFS) and breadth-first search (BFS). When would you use each?
How would you implement a thread-safe singleton pattern? Discuss different approaches.
Design a distributed logging system for a microservices architecture. Discuss data collection, storage, and analysis.
How would you implement a real-time collaborative editing system like Google Docs? Discuss conflict resolution and synchronization.
Explain the CAP theorem and how it affects distributed system design. Provide examples of trade-offs.

roles.title: Web Developer
roles.description: Frontend/backend development, frameworks, APIs, and web technologies. Focuses on UI/UX, responsiveness, and performance.

questions.question: What is the difference between let, const, and var in JavaScript? When would you use each?
Explain the CSS box model and how margins, padding, and borders work together.
What is the Virtual DOM in React and how does it improve performance? Compare it with real DOM manipulation.
How would you implement authentication in a web application? Discuss JWT vs session-based authentication.
Design a responsive e-commerce website architecture. Discuss the frontend framework choice, state management, and performance optimization strategies.
How would you implement real-time features like chat or notifications in a web application? Discuss WebSockets vs Server-Sent Events.
What is the difference between inline, block, and inline-block CSS display properties?
Explain event bubbling and event capturing in JavaScript. How do you handle events efficiently?
What are CSS Grid and Flexbox? When would you use each layout system?
How would you optimize web performance? Discuss lazy loading, code splitting, and caching strategies.
What are React Hooks and how do they differ from class components? Provide examples of custom hooks.
How would you handle state management in a large React application? Compare Redux, Context API, and Zustand.
Design a progressive web app (PWA) architecture. Discuss service workers, offline functionality, and app shell model.
How would you implement a secure file upload system? Discuss validation, storage, and security considerations.
Explain Content Security Policy (CSP) and other security headers for web applications. How do you prevent XSS attacks?

roles.title: Backend Developer
roles.description: You will work on server logic, APIs, and database handling in real scenarios. This section evaluates your ability to build secure and scalable systems.

questions.question: What is REST and how does it differ from SOAP? When would you choose one over the other?
Explain the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases. Give examples of when to use each.
How would you design a database schema for a social media platform? Discuss normalization, indexing, and relationships.
What are microservices and how do they differ from monolithic architecture? Discuss the pros and cons.
Design a scalable payment processing system. Discuss security considerations, transaction handling, and compliance requirements.
How would you implement rate limiting and caching strategies for a high-traffic API? Discuss different approaches and trade-offs.
What is the difference between GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE HTTP methods? When would you use each?
Explain what an API is and the difference between REST and GraphQL APIs.
What is database indexing and how does it improve query performance?
How would you implement user authentication and authorization in a backend system? Discuss JWT, OAuth, and session management.
What are database transactions and ACID properties? Why are they important?
How would you handle database migrations in a production environment? Discuss strategies and best practices.
Design a scalable event-driven architecture using message queues. Discuss Kafka, RabbitMQ, and event sourcing patterns.
How would you implement a multi-tenant SaaS application? Discuss data isolation, security, and scalability challenges.
Explain database sharding strategies and when to use them. Discuss the challenges and trade-offs.

roles.title: UI/UX Designer
roles.description: Design principles, portfolio review, user research, and design thinking for modern product teams.

questions.question: What is the difference between UI and UX design? How do they work together in product development?
Explain the importance of user research in the design process. What methods would you use?
How would you conduct a usability test for a mobile app? Describe the process from planning to analysis.
What is a design system and why is it important for large products? How would you create one?
Design a complete user flow for a food delivery app from onboarding to order completion. Justify your design decisions.
How would you approach redesigning an existing product with poor user experience? What metrics would you track to measure improvement?
What are the principles of visual hierarchy? How do you guide users' attention through design?
Explain the importance of accessibility in design. What are some key accessibility guidelines?
What is responsive design and why is it important? How do you design for different screen sizes?
How would you conduct A/B testing for design changes? What metrics would you track?
What is information architecture? How would you organize content in a complex application?
How do you create user personas and journey maps? What information do you need?
Design a mobile banking app from scratch. Discuss security considerations, user flows, and key features.
How would you measure the ROI of design improvements? What metrics and methods would you use?
What is design thinking? How would you apply it to solve a complex business problem?

roles.title: App Developer
roles.description: Mobile development, platform-specific features, and app deployment across iOS and Android platforms.

questions.question: What is the difference between native and cross-platform mobile development? When would you choose each?
Explain the mobile app lifecycle. What are the different states an app can be in?
How would you handle offline functionality in a mobile app? Discuss data synchronization strategies.
What are the key considerations for mobile app performance optimization? Discuss memory management and battery usage.
Design a mobile architecture for a social media app with real-time messaging, stories, and live streaming capabilities.
How would you implement secure mobile app authentication and data storage? Discuss platform-specific security features.
What are the main differences between iOS and Android development? How do platform guidelines affect design?
Explain app permissions and why they're important for mobile security and user privacy.
What is push notification and how does it work? When should you use it in an app?
How would you implement biometric authentication (fingerprint/face) in a mobile app?
What are the key considerations for mobile app onboarding? How do you design an effective first-time user experience?
How would you handle background tasks and notifications in iOS and Android? Discuss platform differences.
Design a mobile architecture for a food delivery app with real-time tracking, payments, and chat features.
How would you implement mobile app analytics and crash reporting? Discuss tools and best practices.
What strategies would you use to reduce mobile app size and improve download speed?

roles.title: DevOps Engineer
roles.description: CI/CD pipelines, cloud infrastructure, monitoring, and automation for scalable production systems.

questions.question: What is DevOps and how does it differ from traditional software development? Explain the DevOps lifecycle.
What is Docker and why is it useful? How does it differ from virtual machines?
Design a CI/CD pipeline for a web application. Discuss the stages, tools, and best practices.
What is Infrastructure as Code (IaC)? How would you use tools like Terraform or CloudFormation?
Design a highly available microservices architecture using Kubernetes. Discuss service mesh, load balancing, and fault tolerance.
How would you implement comprehensive monitoring and alerting for a distributed system? Discuss observability pillars.
What is version control and why is Git important for DevOps? Explain basic Git workflows.
What is continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD)? Why are they important?
Explain the difference between on-premises, cloud, and hybrid infrastructure.
How would you implement automated testing in a CI/CD pipeline? Discuss different types of tests.
What are containers and how do they differ from virtual machines? Discuss container orchestration.
How would you implement secret management in a cloud environment? Discuss best practices.
Design a disaster recovery strategy for a critical application. Discuss RTO, RPO, and implementation approaches.
How would you implement blue-green deployment and canary releases? Discuss pros and cons.
What is GitOps and how would you implement it using tools like ArgoCD or Flux?

roles.title: Data Scientist
roles.description: Machine learning, statistical analysis, data visualization, and modeling for data-driven decisions.

questions.question: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning? Give examples of each.
Explain the bias-variance tradeoff in machine learning. How do you find the right balance?
How would you handle missing data in a dataset? Discuss different strategies and their implications.
What is feature engineering and why is it important? Give examples of feature engineering techniques.
Design a machine learning pipeline for a recommendation system. Discuss data collection, model selection, and evaluation metrics.
How would you deploy a machine learning model to production? Discuss model serving, monitoring, and retraining strategies.
What is the difference between classification and regression? Give examples of each.
Explain what overfitting and underfitting are in machine learning. How do you prevent them?
What is cross-validation and why is it important for model evaluation?
How would you evaluate the performance of a classification model? Discuss different metrics.
What are ensemble methods? How do random forests and gradient boosting work?
How would you handle imbalanced datasets in classification problems? Discuss different techniques.
Design an A/B testing framework for machine learning models. Discuss statistical significance and implementation.
How would you implement deep learning for computer vision tasks? Discuss CNNs and transfer learning.
What is model interpretability and why is it important? Discuss techniques like SHAP and LIME.

roles.title: Product Manager
roles.description: Product strategy, user stories, roadmap planning, and stakeholder management for tech products.

questions.question: What is the role of a Product Manager? How do you prioritize features in a product roadmap?
How do you write effective user stories? What are the key components of a good user story?
How would you conduct market research for a new product? Discuss different research methods and tools.
Describe a time when you had to make a difficult product decision with limited data. How did you approach it?
Design a product strategy for launching a new fintech app. Discuss market analysis, competitive landscape, and go-to-market plan.
How would you manage conflicting priorities between engineering, sales, and marketing teams? Provide specific examples.
What is a minimum viable product (MVP)? How do you decide what features to include?
How do you gather and prioritize user feedback? What methods do you use?
What is the difference between a product roadmap and a sprint backlog?
How would you measure product success? What key metrics and KPIs would you track?
Describe how you would work with engineering teams to estimate feature timelines.
How do you handle scope creep during product development? What strategies do you use?
How would you pivot a product strategy based on market feedback or competitive pressure?
Design a product launch strategy for a B2B SaaS product. Discuss go-to-market and pricing strategies.
How do you build and maintain relationships with key stakeholders across different departments?

roles.title: HR / Recruiter
roles.description: Screening interviews, candidate evaluation, and behavioral probing with structured notes.

questions.question: What are the key qualities you look for when screening technical candidates? How do you evaluate technical skills?
How do you structure an effective technical interview? What are the different stages and their purposes?
Describe your approach to evaluating cultural fit during interviews. What questions do you ask and what do you look for?
How do you handle situations where multiple stakeholders have different opinions about a candidate? How do you facilitate decision making?
Design a comprehensive hiring process for scaling a tech startup from 50 to 500 employees. Discuss interview stages, evaluation criteria, and metrics.
How would you improve diversity and inclusion in your hiring process? Discuss specific strategies and measurable outcomes.
What questions do you ask to assess a candidate's problem-solving skills during interviews?
How do you evaluate a candidate's communication skills during an interview?
What is behavioral interviewing and why is it effective? Give examples of STAR method questions.
How do you coordinate and schedule interviews with multiple stakeholders? What tools do you use?
What red flags do you look for during reference checks? How do you verify information?
How do you provide constructive feedback to candidates who are not selected?
Design an interview process for senior leadership positions. What evaluation criteria and methods would you use?
How would you measure the effectiveness of your hiring process? What metrics would you track?
How do you handle salary negotiations with candidates? What strategies do you use for compensation alignment?

roles.title: Behavioral Upskilling
roles.description: Improve workplace behavior, professional conduct, and interpersonal skills for better team dynamics.

questions.question: Describe a situation where you had to work with a difficult team member. How did you handle it?
What does professional communication mean to you? How do you ensure effective communication in a remote team?
How do you give and receive constructive feedback? Provide examples from your experience.
Describe a time when you had to adapt to a major change at work. How did you manage the transition?
How would you mediate a conflict between two team members with different working styles and opinions?
What strategies do you use to build trust and rapport with new team members quickly?
How do you handle disagreements with colleagues in a professional manner?
What does professional etiquette mean in a modern workplace? Give examples.
How do you manage your time effectively when working on multiple projects?
Describe your approach to giving constructive feedback to a team member who is underperforming.
How do you handle workplace conflicts between team members you're managing?
What strategies do you use to maintain work-life balance in a demanding job?
How would you improve team morale during a difficult project or organizational change?
Describe how you would handle a situation where you made a significant mistake at work.
How do you build and maintain professional relationships across different departments?

roles.title: Presentation Skills
roles.description: Master public speaking, slide design, and audience engagement for impactful presentations.

questions.question: How do you structure a presentation for maximum impact? What are the key elements?
What techniques do you use to manage presentation anxiety? How do you stay confident?
How do you tailor your presentation style for different audiences (technical vs non-technical)?
What are the principles of effective slide design? How do you avoid common presentation mistakes?
How would you handle a difficult question from the audience during a presentation? What's your strategy?
Describe how you would prepare and deliver a high-stakes presentation to executive leadership.
How do you open a presentation to grab the audience's attention immediately?
What are the key elements of an effective presentation closing?
How do you use body language effectively during presentations?
How do you incorporate storytelling into technical presentations?
What techniques do you use to keep your audience engaged throughout a long presentation?
How do you handle technical difficulties during a live presentation?
How would you design and deliver a training workshop for a large group?
What strategies do you use to persuade skeptical audiences?
How do you measure the effectiveness of your presentations? What metrics do you track?

roles.title: Communication Boost
roles.description: Enhance verbal and non-verbal communication for professional settings and interviews.

questions.question: What is active listening and why is it important in professional communication?
How does body language impact communication? What are some positive body language cues?
How do you tailor your communication style when explaining complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders?
What strategies do you use to ensure your written communication (emails, reports) is clear and effective?
How do you handle communication in cross-cultural teams? What considerations are important?
Describe your approach to communicating bad news or difficult decisions to a team.
How do you ensure your message is clear and unambiguous in professional communication?
What are common communication barriers and how do you overcome them?
How do you use tone and voice effectively in verbal communication?
How do you communicate project progress and setbacks to stakeholders?
What strategies do you use for effective virtual communication in remote teams?
How do you handle communication breakdowns in team projects?
How would you develop a communication strategy for a major organizational change?
What techniques do you use to communicate complex data insights to non-technical audiences?
How do you build a communication culture that encourages transparency and feedback?

roles.title: Leadership Skills
roles.description: Develop leadership qualities, team management, and decision-making abilities.

questions.question: What does leadership mean to you? How do you define a good leader?
How do you motivate team members who are feeling demotivated or disengaged?
Describe your decision-making process when faced with incomplete information or tight deadlines.
How do you delegate tasks effectively? What factors do you consider when assigning responsibilities?
How would you lead a team through a major organizational change or crisis?
What strategies do you use to develop leadership skills in your team members?
How do you set clear expectations and goals for your team?
What is the difference between managing and leading? When do you use each approach?
How do you provide effective feedback that helps team members improve?
How do you handle underperforming team members? What steps do you take?
What strategies do you use to build team cohesion and collaboration?
How do you communicate vision and inspire your team to achieve goals?
How would you develop a leadership development program for your organization?
What is situational leadership and how do you apply it in different scenarios?
How do you balance being approachable with maintaining authority as a leader?

roles.title: Negotiation Skills
roles.description: Learn effective negotiation techniques for business and career advancement.

questions.question: What are the key principles of effective negotiation? How do you prepare for a negotiation?
How do you identify your BATNA (Best Alternative to a Negotiated Agreement) and why is it important?
Describe a time when you successfully negotiated a win-win outcome. What was your approach?
How do you handle negotiations when you have less power or leverage than the other party?
How would you negotiate a salary increase or promotion? What research and preparation would you do?
What techniques do you use to overcome common negotiation tactics like anchoring or good cop/bad cop?
How do you build rapport and trust before entering a negotiation?
What is active listening in negotiation and why is it important?
How do you identify and prioritize your needs vs. wants in a negotiation?
What strategies do you use for negotiating with difficult people?
How do you handle emotional negotiations and keep discussions productive?
What is distributive vs. integrative bargaining? When do you use each?
How would you negotiate a major business partnership or merger? Discuss key considerations.
What ethical considerations are important in negotiations? How do you maintain integrity?
How do you recover from a failed negotiation and maintain relationships?

roles.title: Time Management
roles.description: Master productivity techniques and efficient work habits for peak performance.

questions.question: How do you prioritize your tasks when everything seems urgent? What methods do you use?
What time management techniques have you found most effective? (Pomodoro, time blocking, etc.)
How do you handle interruptions and maintain focus during deep work sessions?
Describe your approach to managing multiple projects with competing deadlines.
How do you maintain work-life balance while working on demanding projects or during crunch times?
What strategies do you use to avoid procrastination and maintain consistent productivity?
How do you create an effective daily schedule? What tools do you use?
What is the Eisenhower Matrix and how do you use it for prioritization?
How do you minimize distractions and maintain focus during work?
What strategies do you use for effective meeting management?
How do you handle unexpected interruptions and urgent requests?
What is time blocking and how does it compare to other time management methods?
How would you implement a time management system for an entire team?
What metrics do you use to measure and improve your time management effectiveness?
How do you adapt your time management strategies when working across different time zones?

roles.title: Stress Management
roles.description: Develop coping strategies and maintain mental well-being under pressure.

questions.question: How do you recognize when you're experiencing stress? What are your early warning signs?
What techniques do you use to manage stress in the moment? How effective are they?
How do you maintain performance and decision-making quality under high-pressure situations?
What boundaries do you set to prevent burnout? How do you enforce them?
How do you help team members who are showing signs of stress or burnout?
Describe your long-term strategy for maintaining mental health and resilience in a demanding career.
What are the physical symptoms of stress and how do you address them?
How do you create a stress-free work environment?
What relaxation techniques do you use during work breaks?
How do you manage stress during high-pressure deadlines or presentations?
What role does exercise and physical activity play in your stress management?
How do you help colleagues who are experiencing stress or burnout?
How would you design a workplace wellness program to reduce stress and improve mental health?
What strategies do you use for maintaining perspective during stressful situations?
How do you balance ambition and drive with stress management and self-care?

roles.title: Networking Skills
roles.description: Build professional relationships and expand your network for career growth.

questions.question: How do you approach networking events or conferences? What's your strategy for making meaningful connections?
What's the best way to follow up after meeting someone new? How do you maintain professional relationships?
How do you network effectively in virtual or remote environments? What tools and platforms work best?
How do you provide value to your professional network rather than just asking for favors?
How would you build a professional network from scratch in a new industry or city?
What strategies do you use to leverage your network for career opportunities without seeming transactional?
How do you prepare for networking events? What research do you do beforehand?
What is an elevator pitch and how do you craft an effective one?
How do you overcome networking anxiety or shyness at professional events?
What platforms do you use for professional networking and how do you optimize your profiles?
How do you transition a casual conversation into a meaningful professional connection?
What information should you include in networking follow-up messages?
How would you organize and host a successful networking event or meetup?
How do you maintain and nurture your professional network over time?
What is the role of mentorship in networking? How do you find mentors and become one?

roles.title: Critical Thinking
roles.description: Sharpen reasoning, assumptions checking, and decision clarity under pressure.

questions.question: How do you identify and challenge your own assumptions when analyzing a problem?
What questions do you ask to evaluate the credibility of information or sources?
Describe your process for breaking down complex problems into manageable components.
How do you recognize and avoid cognitive biases in your decision-making?
How would you analyze a business proposal that looks good on the surface but might have hidden flaws?
What frameworks or mental models do you use to improve your critical thinking and decision quality?
How do you differentiate between facts and opinions when analyzing information?
What questions do you ask to understand the root cause of a problem?
How do you evaluate the strength of an argument or claim?
What is logical fallacy and how do you identify common fallacies in arguments?
How do you use critical thinking when making decisions under uncertainty?
What strategies do you use for creative problem-solving and thinking outside the box?
How would you apply critical thinking to evaluate a business proposal or investment opportunity?
What is systems thinking and how does it apply to complex problem-solving?
How do you develop critical thinking skills in others? What teaching methods work best?
